name: notification-system-expert
description: Notification system expert for React PWA applications with toast, push, in-app notifications, queueing, and persistence

can_do: Set up push notifications with Service Worker Push API
Build in-app notification center with unread count
cannot_do: Send push notifications from frontend (requires backend with VAPID keys)
Guarantee push notification delivery (OS controls delivery)
Access notification history from other apps

react_hot_toast.statement: react-hot-toast provides toast notifications with built-in animations, customizable duration, positioning, and promise support
react_hot_toast.example: toast.success('Saved!'); toast.error('Failed'); toast.promise(saveData(), { loading: 'Saving...', success: 'Saved!', error: 'Failed' });

toast_options.statement: Toast options include duration (milliseconds), position (top-left, top-center, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-center, bottom-right), icon, style, and className
toast_options.example: toast('Message', { duration: 4000, position: 'top-right', icon: '✅', style: { background: '#333', color: '#fff' } });

toast_with_actions.statement: Toasts can include custom JSX with action buttons using toast((t) => <JSX>) pattern, accessing toast ID via t.id for dismissal

Push_API.statement: Push API allows web apps to receive push notifications from server even when app is not running, using Service Worker
Push_API.example: const subscription = await registration.pushManager.subscribe({ userVisibleOnly: true, applicationServerKey: vapidPublicKey });

notification_permission.statement: Notification.requestPermission() requests user permission for notifications, returning 'granted', 'denied', or 'default'
notification_permission.example: const permission = await Notification.requestPermission(); if (permission === 'granted') { /* enable push */ }

VAPID_keys.statement: VAPID (Voluntary Application Server Identification) keys authenticate push server, consisting of public key (frontend) and private key (backend)
VAPID_keys.example: Backend stores VAPID private key, frontend uses VAPID public key in pushManager.subscribe({ applicationServerKey: vapidPublicKey })

push_subscription.statement: PushSubscription contains endpoint URL and keys for server to send push notifications, must be sent to backend for storage
push_subscription.example: const subscription = await registration.pushManager.getSubscription(); await fetch('/api/push/subscribe', { body: JSON.stringify(subscription) });

service_worker_push_event.statement: Service Worker handles 'push' event to display notifications using self.registration.showNotification(title, options)
service_worker_push_event.example: self.addEventListener('push', (event) => { const data = event.data.json(); event.waitUntil(self.registration.showNotification(data.title, { body: data.body })); });

notification_click_event.statement: Service Worker handles 'notificationclick' event to open URLs or perform actions when user clicks notification

notification_center_component.statement: Notification center displays dropdown list of notifications with unread count badge, typically using React Query for data fetching
notification_center_component.example: const { data: notifications } = useQuery('notifications', fetchNotifications); const unreadCount = notifications.filter(n => !n.read).length;

unread_count_badge.statement: Unread count badge shows number of unread notifications on notification bell icon, updating in real-time

mark_as_read.statement: Marking notification as read updates read status via API and invalidates React Query cache to refresh UI

priority_queue.statement: Priority queue orders notifications by priority (CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW) and limits concurrent displayed notifications
priority_queue.example: enum Priority { LOW = 1, MEDIUM = 2, HIGH = 3, CRITICAL = 4 }; queue.sort((a, b) => b.priority - a.priority);

queue_processing.statement: Queue processes notifications sequentially respecting maxConcurrent limit to prevent notification overload

indexeddb_storage.statement: IndexedDB stores notification history with indexes on read status and creation date for efficient querying
indexeddb_storage.example: const store = db.createObjectStore('notifications', { keyPath: 'id' }); store.createIndex('by-read', 'read'); store.createIndex('by-date', 'createdAt');

idb_library.statement: idb library provides Promise-based IndexedDB wrapper for easier async/await usage in TypeScript
idb_library.example: import { openDB } from 'idb'; const db = await openDB('notifications', 1, { upgrade(db) { db.createObjectStore('notifications'); } });

toast_actions.statement: Toast notifications can include multiple action buttons with onClick handlers, dismissing toast via toast.dismiss(t.id)

push_actions.statement: Push notifications support actions array with action, title, and optional icon properties handled in notificationclick event
push_actions.example: actions: [{ action: 'view', title: 'View', icon: '/view.png' }]; // Handle: if (event.action === 'view') { clients.openWindow('/url'); }

notification_settings.statement: User preferences control notification types (toast, push, in-app, email), categories, and quiet hours stored per user
notification_settings.example: interface Preferences { enableToast: boolean; enablePush: boolean; quietHoursStart?: string; categories: { caseAssignments: boolean; } }

quiet_hours.statement: Quiet hours prevent notifications during specified time range (e.g., 22:00-08:00), checked before displaying notifications
quiet_hours.example: if (isQuietHours(preferences.quietHoursStart, preferences.quietHoursEnd, new Date())) { return; /* skip notification */ }

useNotifications_hook.statement: Custom useNotifications hook provides notifications data, unreadCount, markAsRead function, and showToast helper
useNotifications_hook.example: const { notifications, unreadCount, markAsRead, showToast } = useNotifications(); showToast('Saved!', 'success');

react_query_polling.statement: React Query refetchInterval option polls notification API at regular intervals (e.g., 30 seconds) for new notifications

mock_notifications_api.statement: Notification tests mock API calls with jest.mock and verify UI updates using React Testing Library waitFor
mock_notifications_api.example: jest.mock('../api/notifications'); (api.fetchNotifications as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue([{ id: '1', read: false }]); await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText('1')).toBeInTheDocument());

permission_timing.statement: Request notification permission with clear user benefit and context, never on page load without explanation
permission_timing.example: Show dialog explaining benefits: 'Get notified when cases are assigned to you' with 'Enable Notifications' button

notification_frequency.statement: Limit notification frequency to prevent user annoyance, grouping related notifications and using priority queue
notification_frequency.example: Group 5 case updates into single notification: 'You have 5 case updates' instead of 5 separate notifications
